mimicry_chain_detections.reasoning: FCRA Amendment 2026 (tabled March 2026, deferred April 2026) creates Designated Authority to seize NGO assets — functionally equivalent to Russian Foreign Agents Law asset seizure mechanism. Extends existing chain: Russia (2012) → Georgia (2025-2026) → El Salvador (2025) → Kazakhstan (2026) → India (2026). Six countries now operating foreign agent/NGO restriction regimes with asset seizure provisions.

June 2025 judicial elections (Morena swept all 9 SCJN seats, 13% turnout) + INAI abolition (Nov 2024) + February 2026 electoral reform proposal mirrors Hungary's electoral engineering template: structural redistricting + institutional capture + transparency agency elimination. BTI 2026 confirms shift to hegemonic control model (PRI-era pattern).

Constitution revised overnight November 2025. April 12, 2026 election confirmed: 94% outcome for ruling candidate — escalation trigger crossed. Mirrors electoral restriction template: constitutional manipulation + predetermined outcome. Benin and Hungary both experienced overnight constitutional changes followed by engineered electoral results.

March 2026 law package criminalizes foreign grants and establishes extremism law with 8-year civil society ban. Opposition party ban attempts documented. Mirrors Russia-Turkey pattern: foreign agent law + opposition prosecution + civil society restrictions. OSCE Moscow Mechanism (March 12, 2026) confirms torture-level treatment and near-total impunity — institutional weaponization against opposition.

Ekrem Imamoglu (Istanbul Mayor, CHP presidential candidate) targeted with corruption + espionage charges; university degree annulled; media seizure. Judicial weaponization against opposition leader. Establishes template for opposition prosecution chain now observed in Georgia, Nicaragua, and emerging in other jurisdictions.

analyst_briefing: SEVERITY REVIEW SUMMARY: Nine watchlist countries crossed promotion thresholds to RapidDecay status based on convergent authoritative sources (V-Dem 2026, Freedom House 2026, EU Liberties 2026). Italy, United Kingdom, India, Mexico, Bangladesh, Bulgaria, Israel, Colombia, and Côte d'Ivoire all demonstrate 2+ institutional pillars under attack with multiple independent source corroboration. Hungary transitioned from RapidDecay to Recovery following April 12, 2026 election (Tisza supermajority 141/199 seats, 79.56% turnout, Orban concession). Promotion to Recovery bucket appropriate at next cycle post-government formation (May 9 Assembly convenes). Georgia and United States maintained RapidDecay status with no material escalation since baseline re-verification. MIMICRY CHAIN DETECTIONS: Russian foreign agent law template now operates in six countries (Russia, Georgia, El Salvador, Kazakhstan, India, Bosnia-Herzegovina). India's FCRA Amendment 2026 represents direct continuation of chain with asset seizure provisions. Electoral restriction template (Hungary model) replicated in Mexico (judicial capture + INAI abolition) and Benin (overnight constitutional revision + 94% predetermined outcome). Opposition prosecution chain (Turkey model) active in Georgia (opposition party ban attempts) and Nicaragua (all opposition dissolved). WATCHLIST THRESHOLD ASSESSMENTS: Nine countries promoted from watchlist to RapidDecay. No countries demonstrated recovery indicators sufficient for promotion to Recovery bucket. CROSS-MONITOR ESCALATION: Colombia flagged to SCEM (state fragility + democratic erosion + conflict escalation). Hungary flagged to FCW (three confirmed Russian FIMI operations despite electoral resilience). Italy, United Kingdom, Bulgaria flagged to ESA (EU member/post-Brexit democratic backsliding). Georgia and India flagged to AGM (potential AI-enabled civil society targeting). CONTESTED FINDINGS: Uganda, DRC, Iraq marked for human review due to data insufficiency (no material change documented, confidence levels ambiguous, or entry type classification unclear). ANALYST NOTE: April 12, 2026 Hungarian election represents significant democratic resilience signal — structural gerrymandering overcome by electoral wave despite documented FIMI operations. Contrasts sharply with Benin (94% predetermined outcome) and Mexico (judicial capture). Suggests electoral integrity remains contestable in some jurisdictions despite authoritarian institutional engineering.
